riddles.question: I have keys but open no locks. I have space but no room. You can enter, but you can't go inside. What am I?
The more you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
What has hands but cannot clap?
What has a neck but no head?
What gets wetter the more it dries?
I'm tall when I'm young and short when I'm old. What am I?
What has a thumb and four fingers but is not alive?
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner?
What has many teeth but cannot bite?
What has one eye but cannot see?
What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs?
What goes up but never comes down?
What runs all around a yard but never moves?
What has words but never speaks?
What can fill a room but takes up no space?
What gets bigger the more you take away from it?
What has legs but doesn't walk?
What kind of band never plays music?
What has a bottom at the top?
What is full of holes but still holds water?
What month of the year has 28 days?
What word is spelled wrong in every dictionary?
What has to be broken before you can use it?
What is so fragile that saying its name breaks it?
I follow you all day long, but when the sun sets I disappear. What am I?
What can you catch but never throw?
What has a head and a tail but no body?
What has cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and water but no fish?
The person who makes it sells it. The person who buys it never uses it. The person who uses it never knows. What is it?
What goes through towns and over hills but never moves?
What can you keep after giving it to someone?
What has a ring but no finger?
What kind of room has no doors or windows?
What building has the most stories?
What can run but never walks, has a bed but never sleeps?
What invention lets you look right through a wall?
What two things can you never eat for breakfast?
What gets sharper the more you use it?
What flies without wings?
What breaks when you say it?
I roar without a mouth and rush without feet. I can carve through stone given enough time. What am I?
I have a bark but no bite, and I lose my leaves but I'm still alive. What am I?
I fall but I never get hurt, and I'm gone by the afternoon sun. What am I?
I light up the sky for a moment, then thunder follows my name. What am I?
I am bright by day and gone by night, and you must never stare at me. What am I?
I pull the tides and light the night, yet I shine with borrowed light. What am I?
I am a colorful arc that appears after the rain. What am I?
I am made of water but I float in the sky, and from me the rain may fall. What am I?
I have a trunk but I am not a tree, and I never forget. What am I?
I build a web but I am not a spinner of cloth, and I have eight legs. What am I?
I change from a crawler to a flyer, and my wings are full of color. What am I?
I am the tallest animal, and I reach the highest leaves. What am I?
I carry my house on my back and I am never in a hurry. What am I?
I bloom in spring, close at night, and I follow the sun across the sky. What am I?
I am frozen and I float, but most of me hides beneath the sea. What am I?
What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
I start with E, end with E, and contain only one letter. What am I?
What word contains 26 letters but only has three syllables?
Forward I am heavy, backward I am not. What word am I?
What word looks the same upside down and backward?
Remove my first letter and I sound the same. Remove my last letter and I still sound the same. What word am I?
What word has three consecutive double letters?
I am an odd number. Take away one letter and I become even. What number am I?
What four-letter word can be written forward, backward, or upside down, and still be read from left to right?
What begins with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
A man builds a house with all four sides facing south. A bear walks by. What color is the bear?
Two fathers and two sons go fishing. They each catch one fish, yet only three fish are caught. How is this possible?
accepted: a grandfather a father and a son

riddles.question: If there are three apples and you take away two, how many do you have?
A rooster lays an egg on the very top of a slanted roof. Which way does the egg roll?
How many times can you subtract the number 5 from 25?
A girl was born in 1990 and is now 35 years old, but she has only had eight birthdays. How can this be?
accepted: she was born on a leap day

riddles.question: What can you hold in your right hand but never in your left hand?
Before Mount Everest was discovered, what was the tallest mountain on Earth?
What can you put in a bucket to make it weigh less?
If a plane crashes exactly on the border of two countries, where do you bury the survivors?
I am a place where you wash, and I have a head but no body. Turn me on and I rain on you. What am I?
I keep your food cold and hum through the night. Open me and a light turns on. What am I?
You sit on me, but I am not a chair, and I spin you in front of a desk. What am I?
I have shelves and a door, I'm full of clothes, and I stand in your bedroom. What am I?
I sweep the floor while you do nothing, and I roll in circles all on my own. What am I?
I hang on the wall and tell you the time, and my hands go round and round. What am I?
I am the door you walk through to leave or arrive, and I welcome every guest. What am I?
I hold your dreams at night and have four legs, but I never walk. What am I?
I show you your own face, but I am not a photo, and lefts become rights in me. What am I?
I cover your floor, I soften your steps, and you might trip on my edge. What am I?
